sections.paragraph: m. (fem. perhaps, Hos 7:4) an oven, a furnace. Arab. تَنُّورُ (compounded of the unused תַּן a furnace (from the root תָּנַן No. II) and נוּר fire, Exod. 7:28 Levit. 2:4 7:9 11:35 Hos. loc. cit.) As to the ovens of the Orientals which have often the form of a great pot, see Jahn’s Bibl. Archæol. vol. i. 1, P. 213, and 2, page 182. Beckmann’s Beiträge zur Geschichte der Erfindungen, vol. ii. p. 419; compare Schneider and Passow. Lex., v. κλίβανος.
